event.name: event__layout_ready
event.script: this.rawValue = xfa.layout.page(this);

event.name: event__layout_ready
event.script: this.rawValue = xfa.layout.pageCount();

items.text: ( This document is considered Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) when filled. )

event.name: event__change
event__enter
event.script: app.runtimeHighlight = true;

body.p: This form will be destroyed upon: reassignment (other than rehabilitative transfers), separation at ETS, or upon retirement. For separation requirements and notification of loss of benefits/consequences see local directives and AR 635-200.

event.name: event__layout_ready
event.script: this.rawValue = xfa.layout.page(this);

event.name: event__layout_ready
event.script: this.rawValue = xfa.layout.pageCount();

value.text: Note: Both the counselor and the individual counseled should retain a record of the counseling.

event.name: event__layout_ready
event.script: this.rawValue = xfa.layout.page(this);

event.name: event__layout_ready
event.script: this.rawValue = xfa.layout.pageCount();

value.text: PART III - SUMMARY OF COUNSELING Complete this section during or immediately subsequent to counseling.
Note: Both the counselor and the individual counseled should retain a record of the counseling.
5 USC 301, Departmental Regulations, 10 USC 3013, Secretary of the Army.

These records are created and maintained to manage the member's Army and Army National Guard service effectively, to document historically a member's military service,and safeguard the rights of the member and the Army.

body.p: For additional information, see the System of Records Notice A0600-8-104b AHRC, https://dpcld.defense.gov/Privacy/SORNsIndex/DOD-wide-SORN-Article-View/Article/570051/a0600-8-104b-ahrc/.

value.text: There are no specific routine uses anticipated for this form; however, it may be subject to a number of proper and necessary routine uses identified in the system of records notice specified in the purpose statement above.

body.p: For use of this form, see ATP 6-22.1; the proponent agency is TRADOC.

assist.speak: Use the drop down list to select Rank / Grade.
assist.toolTip: Use the drop down list to select Rank / Grade.

event.name: event__click
event__exit
event.script: this.rawValue=this.rawValue.toUpperCase();

assist.speak: Enter name. Last, First, Middle Initial.
assist.toolTip: Enter name. Last, First, Middle Initial.

assist.speak: Enter Name and Title of Counselor.
assist.toolTip: Enter Name and Title of Counselor.

body.p: Purpose of Counseling: (Leader states the reason for the counseling, e.g. Performance/Professional/Event-Oriented counseling, and include the leader's facts and observations prior to the counseling.)

event.name: event__change
event__change
event__change
event__change
event__docReady
event.script: xfa.host.setFocus(form1.Main_Subform.Name);

event.name: event__change

assist.speak: Check for R and I Counseling.
assist.toolTip: Check for R and I Counseling.

event.name: event__change
event__change
event__change
event__click
event__change
event__click
event__change
event__click
event__change
event__change
event__click

value.text: PART III - SUMMARY OF COUNSELING Complete this section during or immediately subsequent to counseling.

body.p: Plan of Action (Outlines actions that the subordinate will do after the counseling session to reach the agreed upon goal(s). The actions must be specific enough to modify or maintain the subordinate's behavior and include a specified time line for implementation and assessment (Part IV below).

assist.toolTip: Specific, measurable, achievable, realistic, time-based objectives and milestones. Enter text.
assist.speak: Specific, measurable, achievable, realistic, time-based objectives and milestones. Enter text.
Check for I disagree with the information above.
assist.toolTip: Check for I disagree with the information above.

event.name: event__change

body.p: Session Closing: (The leader summarizes the key points of the session and checks if the subordinate understands the plan of action. The subordinate agrees / disagrees and provides remarks if appropriate.)

event.name: event__preSign
event__postSign
event__change

assist.speak: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.
assist.toolTip: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.

body.p: Leader Responsibilities: (Leader's responsibilities in implementing the plan of action.)

event.name: event__preSign
event__postSign
event__change

assist.speak: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.
assist.toolTip: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.

value.text: PART IV - ASSESSMENT OF THE PLAN OF ACTION

body.p: Assessment: (Did the plan of action achieve the desired results? This section is completed by both the leader and the individual counseled and provides useful information for follow-up counseling.)

value.text: Purpose of Counseling: Identify and review strengths and weaknesses, create an Individual Development Plan (IDP).

assist.speak: Purpose of Counseling: Identify and review strengths and weaknesses, create an Individual Development Plan (IDP). Enter text.

value.text: PART III - SUMMARY OF COUNSELING Complete this section during or immediately subsequent to counseling.

Include opportunities for civilian or military schooling, future duty assignments, special programs, available training support resources, reenlistment options, and promotion opportunities and considerations.
Soldier-identified strengths, weaknesses, and displayed Army Values:
Leader-identified strengths, weaknesses, and observed Army Values:

body.p: Plan of Action (Outlines actions that the subordinate will do after the counseling session to reach the agreed upon goal(s). The actions must be specific enough to modify or maintain the subordinate's behavior and include a specified time line for implementation and assessment (Part IV below).

body.p: Purpose: To review the duty performance during a specified period (monthly, quarterly, other

value.text: ); to establish performance objectives and clear
standards for the next counseling period; and to identify strengths, areas to improve, and potential.

value.text: PART III - SUMMARY OF COUNSELING Complete this section during or immediately subsequent to counseling.

body.p: Plan of Action (Outlines actions that the subordinate will do after the counseling session to reach the agreed upon goal(s). The actions must be specific enough to modify or maintain the subordinate's behavior and include a specified time line for implementation and assessment (Part IV below). (ETS/Separation date, number of days leave accrued, PTDY authorized?, SFL-TAP progress, Career Skills Program, Family goals, civilian education, understanding benefits, financial planning)

value.text: PART III - SUMMARY OF COUNSELING Complete this section during or immediately subsequent to counseling.

assist.toolTip: Key Point 1 - Establishment of the Golden Triangle (Family, Leader, Buddy). Enter text.
assist.speak: Key Point 1 - Establishment of the Golden Triangle (Family, Leader, Buddy). Enter text.

body.p: Establishment of the Golden Triangle (Family, Leader, Buddy):

assist.toolTip: Key Point 1 - Establishment of the Golden Triangle (Family, Leader, Buddy). Enter text.
assist.speak: Key Point 1 - Establishment of the Golden Triangle (Family, Leader, Buddy). Enter text.

assist.toolTip: Key Point 2 - Organizational history, structure, mission; organizational standards and policies; chain of command/NCO support channel familiarization; key leader contact information. Enter text.
assist.speak: Key Point 2 - Organizational history, structure, mission; organizational standards and policies; chain of command/NCO support channel familiarization; key leader contact information. Enter text.

value.text: Organizational history, structure, mission; organizational standards and policies; chain of command/NCO support channel familiarization; key leader contact information:

assist.toolTip: Key Point 2 - Organizational history, structure, mission; organizational standards and policies; chain of command/NCO support channel familiarization; key leader contact information. Enter text.
assist.speak: Key Point 2 - Organizational history, structure, mission; organizational standards and policies; chain of command/NCO support channel familiarization; key leader contact information. Enter text.

assist.toolTip: Key Point 3 - Soldier programs (Soldier of the Month, BOSS, Soldier & Family Readiness Group); educational and training opportunities. Enter text.
assist.speak: Key Point 3 - Soldier programs (Soldier of the Month, BOSS, Soldier & Family Readiness Group); educational and training opportunities. Enter text.

value.text: Soldier programs (Soldier of the Month, BOSS, Soldier & Family Readiness Group); educational and training opportunities:

assist.toolTip: Key Point 3 - Soldier programs (Soldier of the Month, BOSS, Soldier & Family Readiness Group); educational and training opportunities. Enter text.
assist.speak: Key Point 3 - Soldier programs (Soldier of the Month, BOSS, Soldier & Family Readiness Group); educational and training opportunities. Enter text.

assist.toolTip: Key Point 4 - Security and safety Issues; on/off-duty conduct; off-limits and danger areas. Enter text.
assist.speak: Key Point 4 - Security and safety Issues; on/off-duty conduct; off-limits and danger areas. Enter text.

value.text: Security and safety Issues; on/off-duty conduct; off-limits and danger areas:

assist.toolTip: Key Point 4 - Security and safety Issues; on/off-duty conduct; off-limits and danger areas. Enter text.
assist.speak: Key Point 4 - Security and safety Issues; on/off-duty conduct; off-limits and danger areas. Enter text.

assist.toolTip: Key Point 5 - Personnel procedures; initial and special clothing issue. Enter text.
assist.speak: Key Point 5 - Personnel procedures; initial and special clothing issue. Enter text.

assist.toolTip: Key Point 6 - Support activities, functions, and locations. Enter text.
assist.speak: Key Point 6 - Support activities, functions, and locations. Enter text.

assist.toolTip: Key Point 6 - Support activities, functions, and locations. Enter text.
assist.speak: Key Point 6 - Support activities, functions, and locations. Enter text.

assist.toolTip: Key Point 7 - Foreign nation or host nation orientation, as applicable. Enter text.
assist.speak: Key Point 7 - Foreign nation or host nation orientation, as applicable. Enter text.

value.text: Foreign nation or host nation orientation, as applicable:

assist.toolTip: Key Point 7 - Foreign nation or host nation orientation, as applicable. Enter text.
assist.speak: Key Point 7 - Foreign nation or host nation orientation, as applicable. Enter text.

assist.toolTip: Key Point 8 - Other items of interest as determined by the leader or organization. Enter text.
assist.speak: Key Point 8 - Other items of interest as determined by the leader or organization. Enter text.

event__postSign
event__change

assist.speak: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.
assist.toolTip: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.

value.text: PART III - SUMMARY OF COUNSELING Complete this section during or immediately subsequent to counseling.
Soldier and leader outline crisis and events to date; Soldier expresses positive and negative impacts to self, family, unit, and mission:
Soldier identifies: Accomplished activity relating to the crisis; short-term needs (Soldier, Family, Unit); strengths that could assist while addressing this crisis; available means of assistance:

body.p: Plan of Action (Outlines actions that the subordinate will do after the counseling session to reach the agreed upon goal(s). The actions must be specific enough to modify or maintain the subordinate's behavior and include a specified time line for implementation and assessment (Part IV below).

value.text: Leader reviews all key points with Soldier and identifies elements that may have been missed:

assist.toolTip: Leader reviews all key points with Soldier and identifies elements that may have been missed. Enter text.
assist.speak: Leader reviews all key points with Soldier and identifies elements that may have been missed. Enter text.

value.text: Soldier lists ideas to alleviate the impacts of the crisis and address needs:

assist.toolTip: Soldier lists ideas to alleviate the impacts of the crisis and address needs. Enter text.
assist.speak: Soldier lists ideas to alleviate the impacts of the crisis and address needs. Enter text.

value.text: Soldier identifies any issues of safety:

assist.toolTip: Soldier identifies any issues of safety. Enter text.
assist.speak: Soldier identifies any issues of safety. Enter text.

value.text: Soldier and leader refine the plan of action and next steps:

assist.toolTip: Soldier and leader refine the plan of action and next steps. Enter text.
assist.speak: Soldier and leader refine the plan of action and next steps. Enter text.

event.name: event__change

body.p: Session Closing: (The leader summarizes the key points of the session and checks if the subordinate understands the plan of action. The subordinate agrees / disagrees and provides remarks if appropriate.)

event.name: event__preSign
event__postSign
event__change

assist.speak: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.
assist.toolTip: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.

body.p: Leader Responsibilities: (Leader's responsibilities in implementing the plan of action.)
Resolve schedule conflicts; assist in coordination of resources; advocate for Soldier; connect Soldier to support structure (family, friends):

assist.toolTip: Leader Responsibilities: (Leader's responsibilities in implementing the plan of action.) Resolve schedule conflicts; assist in coordination of resources; advocate for Soldier; connect Soldier to support structure (family, friends). Enter text.
assist.speak: Leader Responsibilities: (Leader's responsibilities in implementing the plan of action.) Resolve schedule conflicts; assist in coordination of resources; advocate for Soldier; connect Soldier to support structure (family, friends). Enter text.

event.name: event__preSign
event__postSign
event__change

assist.speak: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.
assist.toolTip: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.

value.text: PART IV - ASSESSMENT OF THE PLAN OF ACTION

body.p: Assessment: (Did the plan of action achieve the desired results? This section is completed by both the leader and the individual counseled and provides useful information for follow-up counseling.)

value.text: Soldier and leader revisit plan of action: note actions accomplished; assess new/not fully addressed needs; address safety concerns; modify plan of action; plan follow-up (if needed):

assist.toolTip: Soldier and leader revisit plan of action: note actions accomplished; assess new/not fully addressed needs; address safety concerns; modify plan of action; plan follow-up (if needed). Enter text.
assist.speak: Soldier and leader revisit plan of action: note actions accomplished; assess new/not fully addressed needs; address safety concerns; modify plan of action; plan follow-up (if needed). Enter text.

event.name: event__preSign
event.script: xfa.host.messageBox("Your digital signature has the same legal effect and consequences as a hand written signature.\n\nThe individual who signs this digital signature, has the sole authority to clear or delete this digital signature.", "Electronic Signature Notice", 1);

event.name: event__preSign
event__postSign
event__change

assist.speak: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.
assist.toolTip: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.

value.text: PART III - SUMMARY OF COUNSELING Complete this section during or immediately subsequent to counseling.
Subordinate and leader review referred agency(ies) for specified concerns or issues:

body.p: Plan of Action (Outlines actions that the subordinate will do after the counseling session to reach the agreed upon goal(s). The actions must be specific enough to modify or maintain the subordinate's behavior and include a specified time line for implementation and assessment (Part IV below).

value.text: Subordinate and leader determine plan of action with each referred agency(ies):

event.name: event__change

body.p: Session Closing: (The leader summarizes the key points of the session and checks if the subordinate understands the plan of action. The subordinate agrees / disagrees and provides remarks if appropriate.)

event.name: event__preSign
event__postSign
event__change

assist.speak: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.
assist.toolTip: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.

body.p: Leader Responsibilities: (Leader's responsibilities in implementing the plan of action.)
Leader assists Soldier in coordinating assistance; ensures time for Soldier to attend services; Leader verifies quality of service and Soldier's needs are met:

assist.toolTip: Leader Responsibilities: (Leader's responsibilities in implementing the plan of action.) Leader assists Soldier in coordinating assistance; ensures time for Soldier to attend services; Leader verifies quality of service and Soldier's needs are met. Enter text.
assist.speak: Leader Responsibilities: (Leader's responsibilities in implementing the plan of action.) Leader assists Soldier in coordinating assistance; ensures time for Soldier to attend services; Leader verifies quality of service and Soldier's needs are met. Enter text.

event.name: event__preSign
event__postSign
event__change

assist.speak: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.
assist.toolTip: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.

value.text: PART IV - ASSESSMENT OF THE PLAN OF ACTION

body.p: Assessment: (Did the plan of action achieve the desired results? This section is completed by both the leader and the individual counseled and provides useful information for follow-up counseling.)

value.text: Ensure supported agency(ies) are meeting the requirements of the plan of action, and the subordinate is as well.

assist.toolTip: Ensure supported agency(ies) are meeting the requirements of the plan of action, and the subordinate is as well. Enter text.
assist.speak: Ensure supported agency(ies) are meeting the requirements of the plan of action, and the subordinate is as well. Enter text.

event.name: event__preSign
event.script: xfa.host.messageBox("Your digital signature has the same legal effect and consequences as a hand written signature.\n\nThe individual who signs this digital signature, has the sole authority to clear or delete this digital signature.", "Electronic Signature Notice", 1);

event.name: event__preSign
event__postSign
event__change

assist.speak: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.
assist.toolTip: The date is entered automatically once your CAC is authenticated.

value.text: Indicate the iteration of the offense (first, second, third, etc.):

assist.speak: Indicate the iteration of the offense (first, second, third, etc.).
assist.toolTip: Indicate the iteration of the offense (first, second, third, etc.).

value.text: PART III - SUMMARY OF COUNSELING Complete this section during or immediately subsequent to counseling.
Specific instance(s) of substandard performance (explain expectations and how the subordinate did not meet standards/expectations):
Recognize patterns of behavior that may keep the subordinate from meeting the standard:
Identify any personal matters affecting subordinate’s performance:
Is subordinate being considered for rehabilitative transfer or adverse actions, including separation?

event.name: event__change
event__change

value.text: Counselor must inform the Soldier of the impact to types of discharge and benefits, to include educational benefits, IAW AR 635-200, paragraph 1-17.

assist.toolTip: Is subordinate being considered for rehabilitative transfer or adverse actions, including separation? Enter text.
assist.speak: Is subordinate being considered for rehabilitative transfer or adverse actions, including separation? Enter text.

body.p: Plan of Action (Outlines actions that the subordinate will do after the counseling session to reach the agreed upon goal(s). The actions must be specific enough to modify or maintain the subordinate's behavior and include a specified time line for implementation and assessment (Part IV below).

value.text: Specific, measurable, achievable, realistic, time-based objectives and milestones.

assist.toolTip: Specific, measurable, achievable, realistic, time-based objectives and milestones. Enter text.
assist.speak: Specific, measurable, achievable, realistic, time-based objectives and milestones. Enter text.

assist.toolTip: Assessment date timeframe: (monthly, quarterly, custom). Enter text.
assist.speak: Assessment date timeframe: (monthly, quarterly, custom). Enter text.
Check for I disagree with the information above.
assist.toolTip: Check for I disagree with the information above.

event.name: event__change

body.p: Session Closing: (The leader summarizes the key points of the session and checks if the subordinate understands the plan of action. The subordinate agrees / disagrees and provides remarks if appropriate.)

body.p: Assessment: (Did the plan of action achieve the desired results? This section is completed by both the leader and the individual counseled and provides useful information for follow-up counseling.)

body.p: Purpose of Counseling: (Leader states the reason for the counseling, e.g. Performance/Professional or Event-Oriented counseling, and includes the leader's facts and observations prior to the counseling.)
